data.title: Smith & Wesson downgraded to Hold at Craig-Hallum on weaker consumer demand
data.description:
Smith & Wesson (NASDAQ:SWBI) on Friday was downgraded to Hold from Buy at Craig-Hallum, which also slashed its target price on the firearms maker to $13 from $18, a day after it reported its fiscal second quarter results.

The Springfield, MA-based company reported results that missed estimates on both the top line and bottom line, meanwhile, quarterly non-GAAP EBITDAS came in at $18.5 million, compared with $19.3 million last year.

"Weak consumer demand and a lackluster firearm-buying frenzy into this election cycle contributed to mixed results and a large cut to FY25 guidance. In our view, the firearms market has entered a digestion period following years of increased buying patterns (stricter regulation fears in early 2010s, pandemic in early 2020s)," said Craig-Hallum analysts.

That said the capital markets firm saw SWBI continue to take share during the quarter and retain a stronghold in the personal protection category and remained long-term believers in the Smith & Wesson brand and expected continued shareholder returns.

Shares of SWBI have largely stayed put, rising about 0.5% since the start of the year. Seeking Alpha's Quant has rated the company as a Hold, while sell-side analysts recommended the stock as a Strong Buy.

data.title: 17 stocks with rebounding operating margins – Goldman
data.description:
Goldman Sachs highlighted 17 companies where operating margins came under pressure this year but are now expected to rebound next year.

Focusing on margin trajectory, analysts found buy-rated lagging companies where EBIT margins came under pressure in 2024 and are expected to improve in 2025 and included stocks that offer positive sales growth in 2025.

These stocks have at least 10% upside to their price target, and improving operating margins in 2025 compared to 2024:

  • Enphase Energy Inc. (ENPH) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 5% vs. 19%
  • Charles River Laboratories (CRL) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 13% vs. 20%
  • Shoals Technologies (SHLS) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 14% vs. 20%
  • Warner Music Group (WMG) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 13% vs. 17%
  • Match Group (MTCH) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 24% vs. 27%
  • Snowflake Inc. (SNOW) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 5% vs. 7%
  • TripAdvisor Inc. (TRIP) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 5% vs. 8%
  • Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. (CLF) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 0% vs. 2%
  • DoubleVerify Inc. (DV) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 13% vs. 15%
  • ON Semiconductor Corp. (ON) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 28% vs. 29%
  • United Parcel Service Inc. (UPS) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 10% vs. 11%
  • Textron Inc. (TXT) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 9% vs. 10%
  • Avantor Inc. (AVTR) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 16% vs. 17%
  • Nucor Corp. (NUE) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 10% vs. 11%
  • Nike Inc. (NKE) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 10% vs. 11%
  • Topbuild Corp. (BLD) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 17% vs. 17%
  • Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO) – Operating margin 24’ vs. 25’: 23% vs. 23%

data.title: Terreno Realty acquires industrial property in New York for $7.6M
data.description:
• Terreno Realty (NYSE:TRNO) on Friday said it has acquired an industrial property located in Long Island City Queens, New York for a purchase price of about $7.6 million.
  • The property consists of one industrial distribution building containing about 17,000 square feet on 0.4 acres. The property is vacant and the estimated stabilized cap rate is 5.7%.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HubSpot to acquire Frame AI
data.description:
• HubSpot (NYSE:HUBS) on Friday said it has signed an agreement to acquire Frame AI, an AI-powered conversation intelligence platform.
  • "With Frame AI’s capabilities, HubSpot will accelerate its ability to unify structured and unstructured data across the customer journey at scale," the company said.
  • Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ClearBridge Energy Midstream Opportunity Fund posts NAV per share of $55.82 at November end
data.description:
• ClearBridge Energy Midstream Opportunity Fund Inc. (NYSE:EMO) said on Friday its net assets came in at $1.02B as of Nov. 30, and the net asset value per share was $55.82.
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at $41.2M at November-end.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mynaric enters into negotiation for further funding, postpones AGM
data.description:
• Mynaric AG (NASDAQ:MYNA) said on Friday that it has received preliminary notice from its commissioned independent German restructuring expert indicating further future capital need.
  • Based on these preliminary findings, the company has entered into negotiations with its primary lender to resolve upon an extension of the US$16.5 million bridge loans maturing on December 30, 2024, as well as to provide additional capital.
  • The lender's willingness to provide additional funding is contingent on the initiation of a financial reorganization under the German Corporate Stabilization and Restructuring Act (("StaRUG")).
  • At this point, management considers it more likely than not that the lender and the company will agree on additional funding for financial restructuring and that the company will apply for the initiation of StaRUG proceedings.
  • If StaRUG proceedings are opened, this could result in shareholders losing part or all of their investment in the company. If StaRUG proceedings are not opened, the company would have to explore other financing opportunities that are currently not available or in sight.
  • With a view to the latest developments, the annual general meeting of the company, scheduled for December 11, 2024, will be cancelled and postponed.
  • Mynaric AG (MYNA) shares dropped more than 37% in Friday pre-market trading.

data.title: VSee Health partners with Abundabox to launch Abundalife
data.description:
• VSee Health (NASDAQ:VSEE) announced a strategic partnership with AbundaBox to launch AbundaLife, a health record management platform. 
  • “At just $34 per year per patient, this partnership has already attracted eight healthcare facilities in its initial rollout, demonstrating the immense potential for revenue acceleration and broad market adoption," said Imo Aisiku, co-CEO and chairman of VSEE.
  • VSEE shares were up 33.3% in premarket trading on Friday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Orbit’s Power Group reports November bookings in excess of $1M
data.description:
• Orbit International (OTCPK:ORBT) on Friday announced that bookings for its Orbit Power Group for the month of November 2024 were in excess of $1M.
  • Deliveries for these orders have already commenced and are expected to continue through the second quarter of 2026, the company said.
  • Bookings for the month of November were highlighted by orders for VPX power supplies totaling over $700,000.
  • The November VPX bookings contributed to the OPG’s record annual VPX bookings of more than $4.5 million for the eleven months ended November 30, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Prologis downgraded at BMO Capital on valuation concerns
data.description:
Industrial REIT Prologis (NYSE:PLD) was trading lower after BMO Capital Markets lowered its recommendation on the stock, citing its valuation "screens expensive".

PLD was 1.35% down Friday pre-market at $112.25.

"While PLD's 2025 AFFO multiple has contracted 540 bps to 24.1x YTD, we believe it screens expensive relative to the 10-yr yield. We estimate PLD's 2025 AFFO yield (inverse of multiple) at 4.15%, -3 bps below (i.e. premium) to the 10-yr vs. its +147 bps historical spread," analyst John Kim said in a research note.

The company estimates its rents to decline by 3% in Q3 and 2% in the near-term.

"President-Elect Trump's proposed 10% incremental tariffs on Chinese imports adds further uncertainty," said the note.

The investment bank downgraded the stock to Underperform from Market Perform, and reduced the price target to $104 from $120.

The average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating on PLD is Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system grades the stock as Hold.

The party is over for industrial space. Vacancies are moving up. There is a lot of bad news on that front, and we think Prologis estimates are still laughably high for 2026 and 2027, said SA contributor Trapping Value.

"So our forecast is that the consensus NAV estimates should decline to under $110 over the next two years," said the author.

data.title: Omineca announces $2.4M brokered financing of flow-through units
data.description:
• Omineca Mining and Metals (OTCPK:OMMSF) on Friday said it has entered into an agreement with Research Capital in connection with a brokered, best-efforts basis, private placement offering of flow-through units of the company at a price of $0.055 per FT Unit, for gross proceeds of up to $2.4M.
  • The gross proceeds will be used for the ongoing drilling and exploration program currently underway at Wingdam located in the Cariboo Mining District of south central British Columbia, the company said.
  • Each warrant shall entitle the holder thereof to purchase one common share at an exercise price of $0.10 for a period of 36 months from closing.
  • The offering is anticipated to close on or about December 20.
  • Source: Press Release
